title: eriogonum angelense
intro: Eriogonum angelense, also known as Angel's Sage or Angel's Bristlebrush, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, belonging to the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and skin irritations. The health benefits associated with Eriogonum angelense include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed orally or used topically to harness its therapeutic properties.
